mainEntity.name: What is the time limit for GST appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 107 (Appellate Authority): 3 months from communication of order, extendable by 1 month on sufficient cause. Section 112 (Tribunal): 3 months from Appellate Authority order. Missing the deadline requires condonation, which is discretionary.

mainEntity.name: How much pre-deposit is required for GST appeal?

acceptedAnswer.text: Section 107: 10% of disputed tax (mandatory, non-refundable even if appeal succeeds — adjustable against final demand). Section 112: additional 20% of remaining disputed tax. Total pre-deposit capped at 25% of disputed tax amount.

mainEntity.name: Can you help with GST show cause notice response?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. SCN response is often more critical than the appeal — a well-drafted response can prevent the demand order entirely. We draft detailed replies with factual rebuttal, legal arguments, case law, and represent at personal hearings.

mainEntity.name: What is ITC reversal under Rule 42/43?

acceptedAnswer.text: Rules 42 (inputs/input services) and 43 (capital goods) prescribe methods for reversing ITC on items used for both taxable and exempt supplies. Incorrect reversal computation by the officer is a common ground for appeal. We verify the computation and challenge errors.

As of March 2026, GST Appellate Tribunals are operational in select locations. For locations without operational tribunals, the appeal limitation period is preserved. We prepare and file appeals to ensure your rights are protected.

mainEntity.name: What is the success rate of GST appeals?

acceptedAnswer.text: Industry data suggests 30-40% of first appeals result in partial or full relief. The success rate depends on the quality of grounds, supporting documentation, and advocacy. Our triage-and-partial-admission strategy focuses resources on winnable grounds, improving the success probability.
